req.title: KYC refresh (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days; accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four testable behaviours: (1) a successful KYC refresh within the 365-day window sets customer.verified=true, (2) duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key returns the original response without side-effects, (3) an account exactly at the 365-day boundary (last valid day) is still accepted, (4) an account beyond the 365-day window is restricted to inbound-only and a compliant submission that fails compliance returns 403. Tests are designed to be self-contained by using fixtures that provision and teardown customer state, and idempotent by resetting state before each run.

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_success_within_window
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last completed KYC refresh fewer than 365 days ago
Customer account is in active state
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with valid KYC payload and unique Idempotency-Key
Assert HTTP 200 response
Assert response body contains customer.verified=true
expected: Response JSON field verified equals true
Response JSON field refreshed is a recent ISO-8601 timestamp
trace: R-FIN-090: customer.verified=true refreshed on successful submission
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_kyc_refresh_success_within_window(api_client, kyc_customer_within_window):
    customer_id = kyc_customer_within_window["id"]
    token = kyc_customer_within_window["token"]
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())

    payload = <build_kyc_payload>(customer_id)
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
    }

    response = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["verified"] is True
    assert "refreshed" in body
    assert <is_recent_timestamp>(body["refreshed"])

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_idempotency_duplicate_key_returns_original
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last completed KYC refresh fewer than 365 days ago
A KYC refresh has already been successfully submitted with a known Idempotency-Key
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with the same Idempotency-Key and identical payload a second time
Assert HTTP 200 response on second call
Assert second response body is identical to the first response body
expected: Response body matches original response body byte-for-byte
customer.verified remains true without duplicate side-effects
trace: R-FIN-090: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_kyc_refresh_idempotency_duplicate_key_returns_original(api_client, kyc_customer_within_window):
    customer_id = kyc_customer_within_window["id"]
    token = kyc_customer_within_window["token"]
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())

    payload = <build_kyc_payload>(customer_id)
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
    }

    first = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code == 200
    first_body = first.json()

    second = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code == 200
    second_body = second.json()

    assert second_body["verified"] == first_body["verified"]
    assert second_body["refreshed"] == first_body["refreshed"]
    assert second_body.get("idempotent_replay") is True

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_on_day_365_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last completed KYC refresh exactly 365 days ago (last valid day of window)
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with valid KYC payload and unique Idempotency-Key
Assert HTTP 200 response
Assert customer.verified=true
expected: HTTP 200 OK — day 365 is within window (inclusive boundary)
Response JSON field verified equals true
trace: R-FIN-090: accounts beyond refresh window restricted; day 365 is last valid day
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_kyc_refresh_on_day_365_boundary_accepted(api_client, kyc_customer_at_day_365):
    customer_id = kyc_customer_at_day_365["id"]
    token = kyc_customer_at_day_365["token"]
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())

    payload = <build_kyc_payload>(customer_id)
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
    }

    response = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["verified"] is True

    account_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/accounts/{customer_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    )
    assert account_resp.json()["restriction"] is None

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_beyond_365_days_account_inbound_only
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last completed KYC refresh 366 days ago (one day beyond window)
actions: Attempt GET /accounts/{id} to confirm inbound-only restriction is active
Attempt a debit/outbound transaction and assert it is rejected
Assert account restriction field equals INBOUND_ONLY
trace: R-FIN-090: accounts beyond refresh window restricted to inbound transactions only
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_beyond_365_days_account_inbound_only(api_client, kyc_customer_beyond_window):
    customer_id = kyc_customer_beyond_window["id"]
    token = kyc_customer_beyond_window["token"]
    auth_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    account_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{customer_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert account_resp.status_code == 200
    assert account_resp.json()["restriction"] == "INBOUND_ONLY"

    outbound_payload = <build_outbound_transaction>(customer_id, amount=100)
    outbound_resp = api_client.post("/transactions", json=outbound_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert outbound_resp.status_code == 403

    inbound_payload = <build_inbound_transaction>(customer_id, amount=50)
    inbound_resp = api_client.post("/transactions", json=inbound_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert inbound_resp.status_code == 200

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_compliance_failure_returns_403
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer account is within the 365-day window
KYC payload contains data that triggers a compliance rejection (e.g., sanctioned entity flag)
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with non-compliant KYC payload and unique Idempotency-Key
Assert HTTP 403 response
Assert response body contains error code indicating compliance failure
Assert customer.verified remains false
expected: Response body error code equals COMPLIANCE_REJECTED
customer.verified is not set to true
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_kyc_refresh_compliance_failure_returns_403(api_client, kyc_customer_within_window):
    customer_id = kyc_customer_within_window["id"]
    token = kyc_customer_within_window["token"]
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())

    payload = <build_non_compliant_kyc_payload>(customer_id)
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
    }

    response = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 403
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "COMPLIANCE_REJECTED"

    account_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/customers/{customer_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    )
    assert account_resp.json()["verified"] is not True

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_day_366_submission_after_restriction_lifts_restriction
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer account is 366 days past last KYC refresh (INBOUND_ONLY restriction active)
Customer submits a compliant KYC refresh payload
actions: Confirm restriction is INBOUND_ONLY before refresh
POST /kyc/refresh with valid compliant payload and unique Idempotency-Key
Assert HTTP 200
Assert customer.verified=true
Assert account restriction is removed
expected: Account restriction field is null after successful refresh
trace: R-FIN-090: verified=true refreshed after compliant submission; restriction cleared
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_kyc_refresh_day_366_submission_after_restriction_lifts_restriction(api_client, kyc_customer_beyond_window):
    customer_id = kyc_customer_beyond_window["id"]
    token = kyc_customer_beyond_window["token"]
    auth_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    pre_check = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{customer_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert pre_check.json()["restriction"] == "INBOUND_ONLY"

    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = <build_kyc_payload>(customer_id)
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"}

    response = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json()["verified"] is True

    post_check = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{customer_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert post_check.json()["restriction"] is None
